title: User narrows Jobs results with filters

journey.userGoal: Narrow a broad set of Jobs results into a more relevant list.
journey.userContext: The user is browsing jobs and has enough intent to add filter constraints, but may still need to revise or clear them.
journey.trigger: The user opens filter controls or lands on Jobs with filters already active.
journey.startingState: Jobs search is visible with either broad results, active filters, or a restorable filtered context.
journey.successOutcome: The user sees results that reflect the intended filter constraints and understands how to revise or clear them.
happyPath: Return to Jobs results with visible confirmation of active filters.
alternatePaths: Dismiss without applying and keep the previous browsing state.
Clear filters to return to broad browsing.
Correct invalid filter input before applying.
Use existing active filters as the starting point for refinement.
hesitationOrFailurePoints: The user cannot tell whether edits are pending or active.
The user cannot recover from too-narrow filters.
The user fears clearing will remove more than intended.
The user sees no results and lacks a clear recovery action.
recoveryExpectations: Cancel and discard paths should not surprise-apply edits.
Clear paths should communicate their scope.
Invalid input should explain what must change.
Empty results should provide a way to broaden or clear filters.
nonGoals: Specify how filters are persisted internally.
Specify visual styling beyond behavior-relevant hierarchy.

confirmationBaseline.notesMd: Backfilled as the audit baseline for the one-pass UX spec creation/update. This records provenance only; it does not replace UX authority, product confirmation, behavior mappings, or runtime drift ledgers.
